content:
This sounds like a very basic question as I type it, but I couldn't find a way (or an example) to create trait objects in the kernel. I.e. the equivalent of

let box = Box::new(FooStruct { ... }) as Box<dyn FooTrait>;

... where FooStruct implements FooTrait, but with KBox instead of Box. Attempting to do this results in the compiler complaining that

an as expression can only be used to convert between primitive types or to coerce to a specific trait object

What is the correct way to do this?
This topic was moved here from #General > Trait objects? by Benno Lossin.
seems like we haven't implemented that yet, since our KBox isn't the one from std.
at the moment only Arc has implemented that feature, so you might be able to use that instead

adding this to box essentially consists of adding #[cfg_attr(CONFIG_RUSTC_HAS_COERCE_POINTEE, derive(core::marker::CoercePointee))] onto it and maybe some other stuff, Alice could maybe help you with that, since she did the code for Arc.
Let me take a look at how Arc does this, this sounds like a good way to learn more about Rust's arcanes! Thanks for the pointer.
